Petition For (1) Instructions Regarding Annuity Payments During Trust Litigation And (2) Instructions Regarding Interests In Prime Smoked Meats, Inc. And Related Litigation
Off Calendar Jan-06-2026 Continued To May-19-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Caitlyn Andrijich for successor trustee Ryan Andersen (via Zoom).
Eric Fernandez for beneficiary David Granzella, who was also present (via Zoom).
Paul Lee for former trustee Nicolette Granzella, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on: 1) Successor Trustee's Petition for Instructions; 2) David Granzella's Petition to Invalidate Amendment, Remove Trustee, Return Trust Property, and Attorney Fees; and 3) David Granzella's Petition for Remedy for Breach of Trust by Trustee, etc.

With respect to the Successor Trustee's Petition for Instructions, the Court inquired into the circumstances of Lillian Granzella-Boyles, including her capacity and financial situation, and stated additional information would be required before the Court could instruct the trustee regarding continuation or suspension of the monthly annuity payments. The Court indicated it was not prepared to authorize suspension of the payments at this time and stated the payments should continue pending further order of the Court or resolution at mediation.

The Court noted the successor trustee had already been substituted into the related civil litigation pending in Contra Costa County, rendering that issue moot. The Court discussed the request to stay the civil litigation and indicated that a stay pending mediation was reasonable. The Court stated its preliminary intent to authorize the successor trustee to use trust assets to prosecute and defend the civil litigation, subject to further order.

With respect to David Granzella's petitions, the Court received status updates from counsel and discussed the possibility of a global resolution, including participation by counsel in the related civil litigation. Counsel reported mediation was scheduled for April 22, 2026, and the Court encouraged the parties to participate.

The Court stated it would issue a Breslin order and continued all matters to May 19, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To 1) Invalidate Amendment (Prob Code 6104 And 811); 2) Remove Trustee; 3) Return Trust Property Prob Code 850 And 859; 4) Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Oct-29-2025 Continued To Jan-06-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Eric Fernandez for Petitioner David Granzella, who was also present.
Paul Lee for Respondent and Trustee Nicolette Granzella, who was also present (via Zoom).
Daniel Diebert appeared via zoom for Ryan Anderson (temporary trustee).

The order show cause was ordered off calendar.

Nicolette Granzella confirmed she is not objecting to getting removed as trustee. The Court asked if she prefers to resign. Ms. Granzella stated removal is fine. The Court indicated its intent to issue an order removing her as trustee and appointing Mr. Andersen as general successor trustee. The Court ordered Mr. Andersen to give a bond as an individual not named in the trust as successor trustee, by referring to Probate Code section 15602(a)(3) & (b). The Court suggested a drop-off ex parte petition to fix Mr. Andersen's bond.

Mr. Hernandez stated the trust may not require a bond, that there a possibility of two trust accounts.
Ms. Diebert confirmed that there are two accounts, Fidelity Trust account, and a Vanguard account.

The Court orders parties to meet and confer scheduling a mediation.

The Petitions are to be continued to January 6, 2026 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Deleon. Not reported.

Petition To 1) Invalidate Amendment (Prob Code 6104 And 811); 2) Remove Trustee; 3) Return Trust Property Prob Code 850 And 859; 4) Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Aug-20-2025 Continued To Oct-29-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Eric Fernandez for Petitioner David Granzella, who was also present.
Jonathan Weinberg for Respondent and Trustee Nicolette Granzella, who was also present (via Zoom).

The matter came on for hearing on: 1) Petition to Invalidate Amendment, Remove Trustee, Return Trust Property, and Attorney Fees; 2) Petition for Remedy of Breach of Trust by Trustee, to Compel Accounting, for Instructions to Sell Trust Real Property, to Suspend and Remove Trustee, for Appointment of Special Fiduciary, for Denial of Trustee Compensation, for Surcharge, and Attorney Fees; and 3) Order to Appear/Order to Show Cause why respondent should not be suspended as trustee.

The Court inquired of Mr. Weinberg regarding the OSC. Mr. Weinberg stated he had not received it, though he acknowledged receipt of the prior order referencing the OSC. He argued that suspension of the trustee would be counterproductive, as the accounting was underway and nearly complete.

Mr. Fernandez argued that the trustee had failed to timely comply with the Court's November 2023 order requiring an accounting, noting over 265 days had passed without completion. He asserted that the trustee continued to reside in the trust property without paying rent, used trust assets to cover expenses and to prosecute separate litigation in Contra Costa County, and had failed to provide transparency to beneficiaries. He requested suspension of the trustee and appointment of a private professional fiduciary, nominating Ryan Anderson.

Mr. Weinberg responded that his client had been ill earlier in the year and had since diligently worked to gather statements and provide documents to the accountant. He further argued that the Contra Costa litigation was initiated by the decedent and pursued per his wishes, and that it was being handled on a contingency basis. Ms. Granzella addressed the Court, stating she had worked extensively to obtain financial records, prepared a detailed spreadsheet of transactions, and made distributions where legally permissible. She acknowledged residing in the trust property, agreed rent was owed, and explained that she had been caring for her elderly mother while administering the trust.

David Granzella also addressed the Court, expressing frustration over delays, lack of communication, and the emotional toll of prolonged litigation. He emphasized the need for a neutral fiduciary to move the matter forward.

The Court suspended Nicolette Granzella as trustee and appointed Ryan Anderson as temporary trustee. The Court ordered Ms. Granzella to prepare and submit a code-compliant accounting through the hearing date no later than October 15, 2025, to counsel. Mr. Fernandez was directed to prepare and circulate a proposed order. The Court stated it shall issue an Order to Show Cause why Ms. Granzella should not be removed as trustee, set for hearing on October 29, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Hearing on the underlying petitions were continued to the same date.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Court reporter: Sheila Pham, CSR #13293, calendar-la@veritext.com, 415-274-9977 (D204)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Trustee To Produce A Probate Code Compliant Trust Accounting; To Suspend Trustee For Breach Of Trust; To Appoint Ryan Andersen, Lpf, As Interim Trustee; And For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jul-28-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Eric Fernandez appeared in person for and with David Granzella (petitioner).
Jonathan Weinberg appeared via zoom for and with Nicolette Granzella (trustee).

The Court queries Mr. Weinberg at the outset regarding his failure to pay $250 sanctions and proof of payment in compliance with the court's order on April 3rd, Mr. Weinberg stated that he will make the necessary payments by today or tomorrow.

Discussion was held regarding Ms. Granzella, that she is living in the property located in San Francisco, that she is using the trust assets to pay for the utilities and her legal fees, and that she's using the trust assets to benefit her.

Ms. Granzella stated that it was her father's wishes to stay at the property in San Francisco for two years, that her father decided to so his Ferrari to pay for his future medical careand that she's had trouble getting all the information to the accountant due to computer issues.

Mr. Fernandez expressed his concern about Ms. Granzella being the trustee, that she's having trouble simply providing bank statements and other statements to the accountant, and that possibly removing her or suspending her and having a professional fiduciary be appointed.

The Court queries counsel's regarding the Motion to Compel Trustee to Produce a Probate Code Complaint Trust Accounting, and if his intentions were to file it as a motion or a petition, council confirmed that his intentions were to file it as a motion. The matter is taken under submission.

Petition to invalidate amendment remove trustee return trust property and attorney fees is continued to August 20, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Petition for remedy for breach of trust by trustee etc., is continued to August 20, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Petition To 1) Invalidate Amendment (Prob Code 6104 And 811); 2) Remove Trustee; 3) Return Trust Property Prob Code 850 And 859; 4) Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Mar-05-2025 Continued To Apr-02-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Eric Fernandez for David Granzella, Petitioner (via Zoom).

The Court confirmed receipt of the Joint Status Report filed by petitioner and inquired about the expected timeline for the trust accounting. Mr. Fernandez stated that he expects to have a complete accounting by the end of March.

The court noted the absence of the trustee's counsel, Mr. Weinberg. Mr. Fernandez stated that the trustee failed to provide the required statements by the agreed-upon deadline of February 21, and that he had concerns regarding the trustee's delays in administration. Mr. Fernandez requested that the court consider suspending the trustee and appointing a fiduciary under Probate Code Section 17206.

The Court found that it is premature to consider the removal of the trustee at this time but acknowledged concerns regarding the delay. Noting that the trustee agreed to provide the accounting by the end of March, the court continued the matter to April 2, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

The court will issue and Order to Appear to attorney Jonathan Weinberg and trustee Nicolette M. Granzella to appear at the April 2, 2025 hearing. An Order to Show Cause will also be issued for attorney Jonathan Weinberg to explain why he should not be sanctioned in the amount of $250 for failing to appear at today's hearing, pursuant to CCP Section 128.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition To 1) Invalidate Amendment (Prob Code 6104 And 811); 2) Remove Trustee; 3) Return Trust Property Prob Code 850 And 859; 4) Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-22-2025 Continued To Mar-05-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jonathan Weinberg for and with Nicolette Granzella, Respondent (via Zoom).
Eric Fernandez for David Granzella, Petitioner (via Zoom).

Discussions were held regarding accounting issues. Mr. Fernandez noted that the first accounting submitted was incomplete, covering only July 21, 2023 (the date of decedent's death) through December 31, 2023. Mr. Weinberg explained that records up to December 31, 2023 were available, and a full-year accounting is being prepared. The final accounting through December 31, 2024 is expected within 4-6 weeks.

Mr. Fernandez expressed concerns about delays, and noted that the largest estate asset, the family home, remains occupied by the respondent. He suggested a potential motion to compel the sale of the property if no progress is made.

The court ordered both parties to meet and confer by February 5, 2025 to discuss the trust's status and other related issues.

The court continued the matter to March 5, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Mr. Weinberg was directed to file the final accounting by February 26, 2025.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Eric Fernandez and Elisa Liggin (observing) for David Granzella, Petitioner (via Zoom).
Jonathan Weinberg for Nicolette Granzella, Respondent (via Zoom).

The court noted that mediation had not occurred. Mr. Fernandez explained that Mr. Weinberg had initially agreed to provide accounting but has not been responsive since the previous hearing.

Mr. Weinberg acknowledged the delay in providing the accounting, which he said has been delayed due to personal issues. Mr. Weinberg stated that an accounting was in progress and that he will provide it whether they go to mediation or not.

Discussion was held regarding a related petition filed by Mr. Granzella in case PTR-24-307818 and petitioner's request to consolidate the two matters. Mr. Weinberg stated that he does not object to consolidation. The court agreed to sign a consolidation order, designating PTR-24-307006 as the lead case.

The court vacated a December 11, 2024 hearing date for the new petition filed in PTR-24-307818 and set both matters for hearing on January 22, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204. Status on mediation is ordered off calendar.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Nov-27-2024 Off Calendar

Petition To 1) Invalidate Amendment (Prob Code 6104 And 811); 2) Remove Trustee; 3) Return Trust Property Prob Code 850 And 859; 4) Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Aug-07-2024 Continued To Nov-27-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Eric Fernandez appeared by zoom for and with David Granzella (petitioner) by zoom.
Jonathan Weinberg appeared by zoom for and with Nicolette Granzella (trustee) by zoom.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than November 13, 2024. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by November 15, 2024. The Petition to Invalidate Amendment, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on November 27, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The parties are to obtain a copy of the mediation order from the court's website.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Belen. Not reported.

Petition To 1) Invalidate Amendment (Prob Code 6104 And 811); 2) Remove Trustee; 3) Return Trust Property Prob Code 850 And 859; 4) Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jun-12-2024 Continued To Aug-07-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Eric Fernandez appeared by zoom for David Granzella (petitioner).
Paul Lee appeared by zoom for Nicolette Granzella (respondent).

Mr. Lee stated that he is in the process of filing his objections and requested a referral to pro bono mediation. Mr. Fernandez said no to mediation, requested that written objections be filed so that the matter is at issue and stated that discovery needs to be propounded. The Petition to Invalidate Amendment, etc., is continued to August 7, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Objections are to be filed by July 10, 2024. If objections are not filed by then, objections are waived. The parties are to file a joint status report by July 17, 2024 indicating what discovery is needed before mediation and when they think they will be ready for mediation.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Petition To 1) Invalidate Amendment (Prob Code 6104 And 811); 2) Remove Trustee; 3) Return Trust Property Prob Code 850 And 859; 4) Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar May-01-2024 Continued To Jun-12-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Cerron Wilkins appeared by zoom for David Granzella (petitioner) by zoom.
Jonathan Weinberg appeared by zoom for and with Nici Granzella (trustee) by zoom.

Mr. Weinberg stated that he has not filed his objections because of the issues that need to be cleared in the case, and in the event the petition needs to be amended. Mr. Weinberg also stated that he does not think there is an issue with venue at this time. The court noted that the response to the examiner's notes have not been verified and informed Mr. Wilkins that they need to be. Mr. Weinberg stated that he was not served with the response to the notes. Mr. Wilkins that if they have not served it, he will make sure that they do and that he will file a proof of service. The parties indicated that they have not discussed mediation yet. The Petition to Invalidate Amendment, etc. is continued to June 12, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The issue of pro bono mediation is to be discussed at that time.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.
